feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Process Automation
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Process Automation Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Unable to subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi Team,   

I have a trail account in SAP BTP and unable to subscribe to build process automation with the free plan. Please help on this.

I'm currently facing issues creating a subscription for SAP Build Process Automation; it's giving me an error message saying the below message.

"Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.”
I have attached a screenshot of the error for your reference.

Please, could you assist me in resolving this issue? I appreciate your help in advance!

Best regards,

entry.title: Subscription failed for SAP build process automation
entry.content:
I am trying out free trial, but when I tried to subscribe to "SAP Build Process Automation" it failed with a message "Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community." tried four times and failed all the time.
I am following steps as per the tutorial. Is there any prerequisite to be able to get it working?

entry.title: "IMPORT FAILED" error - Can't import project from Build Apps standard plan
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We upgraded from Build Apps free plan to Build Apps standard plan following this: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/upgrade-to-sap-build-apps-standard-edition?q=import

We had 56 projects in the free plan, that we exported as recommended in order to import them back to Build Apps standard plan once installed.

However, instead of having 0 project in the brand new standard plan like in the tutorial video, we noticed that 19 projects were already listed there. We tried to import a project that was not in the list but we keep having the following error message:

IMPORT FAILED
Project eu10.yjdhdjdj… already exists
Correlation Id: 748h67….

We saw the following note but unless mistaken it seems to suggest no solution: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003521702

Just contacted SAP Support about it but if anyone of you knows how to fix this, that would be really appreciated.

Thanks,

BR,

LM.

entry.title: Issue with BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 – ITM_NUMBER error in SAP BPA
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m working with SAP Build Process Automation, and I’m trying to create a sales order using the BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2.

However, I consistently receive the following error:

BAPI exception: Parameter/field ITM_NUMBER doesn't have a correct data type

I’ve tried all of the following approaches to set the ITM_NUMBER field in the ORDER_ITEMS_IN structure:

  • Using a string with leading zeros: "000010"

  • Padding the string in JavaScript like this:
    itmNumber.toString().trim().padStart(6, '0')

  • Hardcoding the value directly as "000010"

  • Confirming that it’s a string, not a number

  • Making sure it's exactly 6 characters long with no extra spaces

  • Logging the output right before the BAPI call to ensure it’s correctly formatted

  • Creating a variable of type ANY in SAP BPA and assigning the value "000010"

  • Trying the value without leading zeros, i.e., just "10"

I’ve also verified that all other mandatory fields in ORDER_ITEMS_IN (such as MATERIAL, TARGET_QTY, TARGET_QU, PLANT) are filled in with valid values.

Despite these efforts, the same error persists.

Has anyone encountered this issue before? Could this be related to how SAP BPA structures the call to the BAPI or expects the table input? 

@BorisAndree Any suggestions or examples of working configurations would be greatly appreciated. Thank you in advance!

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Not able to install Desktop Agent - Integrity Issue
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am facing issues with installing desktop agent for SBPA. It is giving the below error - 

I have tried reinstalling the agent, uninstalling and installing the agent, restarting my system but still the issue persists.

Any help would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance, Ketan

entry.title: Build process automation subscription failed in trial account
entry.content: I am trying to subscribe to the free trial of build process automation in my BTP trial account  for region US East (VA) but the subscription is failing.

entry.title: Facing Subscription Challenges in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi All,

I’ve been facing a issue for the past three days while trying to subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation in my Trial account. Despite multiple attempts, the subscription consistently fails.


Has anyone else encountered a similar issue? If so, could you please share your insights or provide a solution that worked for you?

Any help or suggestions would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance for your support!

SAP Build Process Automation 

SAP BTP cockpit 


@Dan_Wroblewski 
@Archana

entry.title: BTP Trial Account: error "Unable to recognize the artifact type"
entry.content:
I am using a BTP Trial Account to prepare for the Low Code/No Code Certification.

I was previously (a few weeks ago) able to create Processes in my Trial Account without any issues.
Now, when trying to create a new Process or edit one which was already created, I am faced with this constant error popup :

"Request failed with status code 400
Unable to recognize the artifact type [notificationtemplate]"

I haven't edited the default 'notificationtemplate' - I don't even know where to check that or how we would edit it. Upon checking SAP For Me I could not find anything related to artifact type errors in BTP / SAP Build. The closest I could find were issues relating to the Cloud Connector.

So, I checked in my Trial Account > Connectivity > Cloud Connectors and I can see:

There is no cloud connector connected to this subaccount.


Request Details
statusCode:404
reason:Not Found
request:/api/v1/subaccount/497134c1-e390-4384-b763-9e02eee8159e/info
timestamp:26.04.2025 12:31:33
response:<empty>

I hadn't previously configured a Cloud Connector for this account and I don't know if it's required, but I was previously able to create Process Automations in the account. If there was a Cloud Connector before I am assuming it was a 'default' Cloud Connector administered by whoever takes care of Trial Accounts in general.

I have tried to contact Support but to do so it requires selecting a customer, and I do not have one - I am an SAP Employee and am using this account to prepare for the Certification. So I cannot contact Support through any standard channels

I cannot enable the Built In Support in BTP Cockpit due to below error.

 @SAPSupport any suggestions on how I can resolve this issue?

Thanks

entry.title: Error "Unable to recognize the artifact type [notificationtemplate]" when creating a form in Process
entry.content:
I encountered the following error while working in SAP Build Process Automation:

"Request failed with status code 400. Unable to recognize the artifact type [notification template]."

Previously, I could create forms and use them normally. However, now when I create a form, it only appears in the Overview section and does not show up in the Process section, so I cannot add it into my process.

Thank you very much for your support!

entry.title: Issue in SAP Build Process Automation action sending empty list payload to SAP CAP action.
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We are building a SAP CAP application where we define an action to store list data into a HANA DB after an approval step in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA).

Here is the setup.
My CAP application schema looks like this.
entity OTPDA : managed {
key userID : String;
issueDate : String;
payComponent : String;
payComponentNature : String;
amount : String;
currency : String;
unit : String;
tmu : String;
assignmentNumber : String;
comments : String;
log : String;
}


CAP action in service section
action storeOtpdaData(OtpdaDatas : array of OTPDA) returns String;

and its handler

const onStoreOtpdaData = async (req) => {
const { OTPDA } = cds.entities("db.payroll");
const tx = cds.transaction(req);
const aOtpdaDatas = req.data.OtpdaDatas;

try {
for (const entry of aOtpdaDatas) {
await tx.run(
UPSERT.into(OTPDA).entries({
userID: entry.userID,
issueDate: entry.issueDate,
payComponent: entry.payComponent,
payComponentNature: entry.payComponentNature,
amount: entry.amount,
currency: entry.currency,
unit: entry.unit,
tmu: entry.tmu,
assignmentNumber: entry.assignmentNumber,
comments: entry.comments,
log: entry.log
})
);
}
return `${aOtpdaDatas.length} record(s) stored successfully.`;
} catch (error) {
console.error("Error inserting OTPDA data:", error);
req.reject(500, "Failed to store OTPDA records.");
}
};

In SAP Build Process Automation, I have created an action using the CAP destination and mapped it accordingly. However, when BPA tries to call the CAP action, the request payload is going blank, and therefore no records are being stored.

Here is the screenshot:- The request section payload structure is array of object  but the request body which BPA is sending is array of blank objects. :-


Any insights or guidance would be highly appreciated.
Thanks a lot in advance!

SAP Build Process Automation SAP Cloud Application Programming Model SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation (trial) instance could not be created
entry.content:
hello,

I want to create a SAP Build Process Automation instance in my trial account.

When trying to create it fails with "Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community."

I've tried it several times in the last days, always same result.

Is there any solution to get SAP Build Process Automation running again?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: BTP Build Process Automation Job Status Remains As Ready
entry.content:
Hello,

We are experiencing a problem in our BTP Build Process Automation system. The Job we created remains in the "Ready" state and gives an error because it is not progressing. The job cannot be run. However, when the same automation is tested manually, it works without any problems. The automation is deployed in "Public" and scheduled as "Scheduled" type. Destination connection and Environments connections have been established successfully. There is no input in the automation, data is received with the "Call Web Service with Destination" tool and Automation is selected as the Scheduler operator.

Detailed information is provided as follows. I kindly request your support for the solution of the problem.

We want to set up a job for the automation we created on SAP BTP Build Process Automation. The job we created remains in "Ready" status, and because the status does not progress, the job gives an error after 1 day and does not work. However, when the automation is run manually for testing, it works without any issues.

1- Error displayed on screen
When we checked for the "Maximum number of concurrent unattended execution reached" error shown in the job, we found that there is only one Agent.

2- Job Setup Process
The created automation has been deployed in "Public".

A scheduler has been set up with the Scheduled type for the deployed project.

The Destination connection has been successfully established. The connection for Environments has been set up.

3- Scheduler Details
We do not have any inputs for the Automation. Data is obtained using the "Call Web Service with Destination" tool.

For the scheduler runner, Automation has been selected.

4- Automation Details
In the first 13 steps of our application, there are processes for retrieving and filtering appropriate data. In the subsequent processes, appropriate operations are performed on the screens we recorded. When the Automation is run manually, it works without any issues.

Step 1

Step 2


Step 3

entry.title: Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post
entry.content:
Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation. Try again. If the problem persists, please post

Please help me to resolve this issue

entry.title: SAP Build Process – Stuck at invoiceRead Step in Invoice Processing Mission
entry.content:
Hi all, I’m following the "Process and approve your invoices with SAP Build Process Automation" mission from SAP Discovery Center.

Link: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/protected/index.html#/mymissiondetail/145344/cardName/Invoice%20automatically%20processed%20and%20posted%20to%20SAP%20S%2F4HANA%20Cloud/?tab=projectboard

My process is getting stuck at the invoiceRead step—it starts but never completes, so it doesn't proceed to the condition branch. When I test the invoiceRead automation in editable mode, it works fine. However, when I run the process in deployed mode, it gets stuck at this step.

I've attached logs and screenshots showing the issue. Any ideas on how to resolve this?


I need the invoiceRead step is to be completed like the below step. Please help me in this case.

entry.title: Build Process Automation - mail attachment size
entry.content:
Is there a way to get information about mail attachment size? Currently my workaround is to save attachments and then use file system activities to retrieve the size. Is there any direct way to get this information from outlook without the need of saving the files (and deleting them later if needed)? Probably using some custom script?

Thank you.

entry.title: “quota exceeded" error in SAP Build basic trial environment
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I am currently preparing for the C_LCNC_2406 (SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer - SAP Build) certification, which requires completion of the "Experiencing End-to-End SAP Build" system exercise ( https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/experiencing-end-to-end-sap-build ).
As part of this exercise, I am instructed to create a new process in SAP Build Process Automation using the provided SAP Build basic trial environment. Unfortunately, when attempting to create a new Process there, I encounter the following error:

Error

Permitted quota of 750 was exceeded (type: "project").

Correlation Id: e6afe57f-1cc0-47b7-64c8-762ce904183e

Has anyone experienced this issue before and knows how I can approach or resolve it?

I’ve already created a case in the SAP Learning help center, but the SAP Learning Support Team informed me that this issue lies outside their support scope as the trial environment is publicly hosted.

I truly appreciate any help or suggestions you can provide.

Best regards,
Anna

entry.title: how to get participant handbook for codejam re: event based processes
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm currently following a codejam blog regarding event-based processes for sap build. here is the link of my current lesson: 3 - Explore SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh | SAP Tutorials

one of the prerequisite is a "participants handbook" which contains the system and credentials needed to follow the blog.

where can i get this? thanks

entry.title: SAP Build Library and SAP Build Apps Integration Issue
entry.content:
Hi guys,
I have one demo process in my SAP Build Process Automation Application and I have deployed and published it.


Now, in my SAP Build Apps, I have created an application and in Integration tab, I selected SAP Build Library, I can see my process there, but when I click on it shows Page not found.


I have also configured sap_process_automation_service_user_access destination in my BTP for the instance of Build Process automation and test shows successful & 401. Also, added that destination in control tower's destination in build lobby. Please help.

In the attached image we can see the published process available in cloud library.

SAP Build Library

Page Not Found Error for process

Thanks & Regards,
Marsh

entry.title: How to create Parallel branches in SAP BTP Process Visibility
entry.content: I need to create parallel branches in SAP BTP Process visibility without creating process orchestration using build process automation as complete process will be running in other application we just need to monitor the process using process visibility.

entry.title: Problem with Action Project Design | Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Builders,

I have discovered a problem with the Action Project Design in the SAP Build Lobby.

I have been trying to structure a request body using the "Build from Scratch" option. However, when I save the fields, items in nested objects will change order. I deleted the fields and added them again and even made an entirely new Action Project with no success in resolving the order of the fields. I assume there is a logic problem with this tool.

In my case I need to have control over the field order or the request will cause a processing failure.

Any help in getting a this resolved would be greatly appreciated.

#SAPBuild

@SAPSupport @Dan_Wroblewski
